anchor.note: P/B is the industry standard for multifinance companies. The value driver is ROE vs Ke -- the spread determines the justified premium or discount to book. No EV/FCF or EV/EBITDA anchor used because FCF is not meaningful in a receivables-growth business.

street.thesis: Bulls: sub-2% NPF (best-in-class), 9%+ new financing growth, 10%+ dividend yield durable; justified P/B above 1.0x if ROE holds and credit costs normalize. Bear (UBS): credit cycle turn driven by Indonesia's shrinking middle class, rising cost of credit, higher funding costs will compress ROE to 12-13%.
street.ubs_note: UBS downgraded to Sell at PT 745 on August 29, 2025 -- the only sell in the panel. Cut estimates 8-29% for 2025-27, forecasting credit costs of 4.6-5.1% and loan CAGR of 2.5%. This is 30% below then-consensus and is the dominant bearish data point in the analyst tape.

history.anchor_cagr_note: NI CAGR FY2022-FY2025: -4.4% p.a. (NI declined from 1,807B to 1,582B as provision spike and GP margin compression offset revenue growth). Revenue CAGR over same period: +8.9% p.a. (5,142B to 6,646B). The NI decline while revenue grew 29% is the crux.

peers.name: Adira Dinamika Multi Finance
peers.note: Bank-backed (Danamon/MUFG); NI fell 27% in 2024 -- far worse than BFIN; 0.64x P/B reflects the worse ROE (12.3%) and weaker earnings trend. Direct competitor in used cars and multipurpose.

peers.name: Clipan Finance Indonesia
peers.note: Subscale, low ROE (4%), very low P/B. Not relevant as a valuation benchmark -- is a distressed/subscale peer. The 0.25x P/B reflects value destruction (ROE well below any reasonable Ke).

peers.name: Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BRI)
peers.note: Deposit-taking bank; lower cost of funds gives BRI structural advantage over multifinance. At 1.32x P/B with ROE 18.1%, BRI is a ceiling reference for BFIN -- BFIN should trade at a discount to BRI given its wholesale-funded model and smaller scale. But BFIN's ROE of 14.8% (vs BRI's 18.1%) at 0.93x P/B (vs BRI's 1.32x) suggests BFIN is cheap relative to the ROE gap.

peer_note: Peer median P/B of 0.64x (ADMF only, excluding the distressed CFIN). BFIN at 0.93x trades at a 45% premium to ADMF -- justified by BFIN's superior NPF (1.25% vs sector 2.70%) and higher ROE (14.8% vs ADMF 12.3%). The premium is earned and historically consistent.
contested_lever: Ke (cost of equity) and ROE normalization trajectory. The market prices Ke at ~14% (crisis), we use 10.5% (fundamental). The market prices no ROE recovery (ROE stays ~13%); we price recovery to 16.5% by FY2028. The call resolves over 12-18 months as BI rate cycle clarifies and H2 2026 earnings show whether provision is normalizing.
